verbatim_text: So what is our goal? Our goal is to take this 4 billion parameter model on the bottom left corner, the 4B thinking model, and train it using techniques that we saw such that when it adapts at test time, that is when it produces more tokens at test time, it can improve performance and match the performance of 600B plus models. And this goal is in some sense a bit ambitious because we only have 5,000 training problems to make this happen. That's a pretty small set for this particular task. The way we do this is we run RL training with the meta RL recipe that we just saw. It uses some notion of the surrogate reward and the sampler that we saw. And I'll get into the details of what that is. But when we train it with the meta RL recipe, with the writes, reward and sampler, then what we get is this QED Nano model. It's a 4 billion parameter model that can match its performance with GPT OSS 120B and QN3 235B thinking. So its performance matches with these two. Now, what is the sampler and the reward we used here? Roughly, if you recall, the right sampler is the one that uses auxiliary data. And the right choice of auxiliary data that we used here is looking at summaries of past proof attempts on a given problem. That's what we used to bootstrap exploration during RL training.
